common.dataPolicySettingsSection.codingA: Privacy and terms for agents that run on your subscription.
common.dataPolicySettingsSection.notes.c: Pro / Max / Team / Enterprise: not used to train Anthropic models by default. Free tier: opt-in for training; treat as 'allows training' if signed in to a Free account.
ChatGPT Plus / Pro / Team / Enterprise / Edu: not used to train OpenAI models. Free ChatGPT: training is on by default unless disabled in Data Controls.
common.dataPolicySettingsSection.notes.o: Unpaid-account content may be used to improve OpenCode services. Third-party models follow their own providers' retention policies. Zen states most providers are zero-retention / no-training, but lists free-model and OpenAI / Anthropic retention exceptions.
common.dataPolicySettingsSection.perProv: Privacy and terms for providers you connect with your own API key.
Informational summary of current data-handling safeguards. Policy changes are managed centrally and audit-logged. This is an internal risk signal, not legal advice.
Only aggregated incident bundles with representative samples and evidence references are sent to providers.
Unredacted diagnostic payloads are blocked unless explicitly enabled by platform policy and audit controls.
common.dataPolicySettingsSection.policyM: Data handling and redaction controls are managed by platform policy and cannot be changed from settings.
common.dataPolicySettingsSection.redacti: Redaction policy version and payload class are recorded for every outbound request.
API keys, secrets, and token-like strings are masked before provider invocation.
Signal IDs and source references are kept, but sensitive field values are masked.
Request size and event count limits are enforced to prevent accidental bulk transfer.
Email addresses, hostnames, and direct personal data are redacted.
common.desktopAbout.superterminalRunsLoc: SuperTerminal runs local security workflows with Electron main-process services and an embedded SQLite data store.

common.tourIncidentsPreview.describeTheS: Describe the security issue to investigate...
common.tourResultsPreview.selectAStepToI: Select a step to inspect its output.
common.tourRunbooksPreview.autoSavesOnBl: Auto-saves on blur, Done, and drag-drop
common.tourRunbooksPreview.title: Title
common.tours.dashboard.description.addAS: Add a source from Settings, sync, and your triaged diagnoses appear here. Replay this tour anytime from Help.
common.tours.dashboard.description.conne: Connect external sources and triage diagnoses from one place.
common.tours.dashboard.description.eachR: Each row is an incident the system has triaged from the synced data. Open one to see severity, environment, and the underlying telemetry.
common.tours.dashboard.description.pickW: Pick which connected service to sync. Your last selection is remembered automatically the next time you open the dashboard.
common.tours.dashboard.description.pullT: Pull the latest issues from the selected source on demand. Filtering happens by log-level threshold so you can keep the volume manageable.
common.tours.dashboard.description.theDa: The dashboard pulls in error and log data from your connected services and turns it into diagnoses you can act on.
common.tours.dataSources.description.cho: Choose External Source as the action type when a runbook needs to query Sentry, Wazuh, or PostHog.
common.tours.dataSources.description.ent: Enter the source name, token, organisation, and project details. Secrets are stored by the desktop app rather than in the runbook.
common.tours.dataSources.description.ext: External Sources are reusable connections that runbooks can query during incident response.
common.tours.dataSources.description.lea: Learn how to connect an External Source and use it from a Runbook.
common.tours.dataSources.description.pic: Pick the provider type that matches the telemetry source you want to query.
common.tours.dataSources.description.que: Queries can combine Global Variables like ${globals.environment} with run-specific parameters such as host.
common.tours.dataSources.description.sel: Select one of the External Sources already connected in Settings.
common.tours.dataSources.description.sta: Start from Settings by adding an External Source. This keeps the connection reusable across runbooks.
common.tours.incidents.description.choos: Choose which LLM provider and model powers the investigation. Configure providers in Settings.
common.tours.incidents.description.click: Click to rename the incident. Titles auto-generate from your first message if left blank.
common.tours.incidents.description.descr: Describe the incident here. Use the + menu to attach images or toggle extended thinking for deeper analysis.
common.tours.incidents.description.eachR: Each runbook execution is listed with its name, status, and step progress. Click one to inspect it.
common.tours.incidents.description.learn: Learn how to drive an AI-assisted incident resolution.
common.tours.incidents.description.resol: Resolve a production incident with an AI agent. Each incident is a conversation: describe the problem, and the agent reads logs, runs runbooks, and reports back what it found.
common.tours.incidents.description.seeLi: See live steps, execution parameters, input/output, and errors for the selected runbook execution.
common.tours.incidents.description.sendY: Send your message to start or continue the investigation. You can also press Cmd+Enter.
common.tours.incidents.description.shows: Shows the investigation status: Idle, Running, Completed, or Failed.
common.tours.incidents.description.start: Start a new AI-assisted investigation. Describe what's happening in production and the agent will look into it.
common.tours.incidents.description.viewA: View all your incidents in a list. Each row shows the title, preview, status, and timestamp.
common.tours.incidents.description.whenT: When the AI triggers runbooks during an investigation, their execution results appear in a side panel. Click to open it.
common.tours.incidents.description.youCa: You can replay this tour anytime from the Help section in Settings.
common.tours.main.description.aQuickWalk: A quick walkthrough of the on-call workspace.
common.tours.main.description.bitSentryI: BitSentry is an on-call workspace for resolving production incidents: connect your log sources, drive AI-assisted investigations, and run deterministic runbooks the same way every time.
common.tours.main.description.buildDeter: Build deterministic runbooks — shell commands, AI prompts, HTTP calls, and external source queries chained together so the same incident response runs the same way every time.
common.tours.main.description.connectLLM: Connect LLM providers, attach external sources like Sentry or PostHog, and replay any tour from the Help section.
common.tours.main.description.everyRunbo: Every runbook execution is captured here with per-step status, input, and output for review and post-mortems.
common.tours.main.description.replayThis: Replay this tour anytime from the Help section in Settings.
common.tours.main.description.resolveALi: Resolve a live incident with an AI agent. Describe what's happening; the agent reads your logs, drives runbooks, and summarizes root cause without leaving the chat.
common.tours.results.description.eachSte: Each step is listed with its type (Shell, AI, HTTP, External) and status. Click a step to inspect it.
common.tours.results.description.everyEx: Every execution is captured for review. Replay this tour anytime from Settings.
common.tours.results.description.everyRu: Every runbook execution lands here with full step-by-step output for review and post-mortems.
common.tours.results.description.jumpToT: Jump to the source runbook to edit or re-run it.
common.tours.results.description.learnHo: Learn how to review what each runbook execution did.
common.tours.results.description.showsTh: Shows the runbook name, status, completed steps, parameters, and final output for this execution.
common.tours.results.description.viewThe: View the full execution history list with stats for Total, Running, Completed, and Failed counts.
View the selected step's input, output, and any errors. Output renders as markdown.
common.tours.runbookCreation.description: Choose Shell, AI, HTTP, or External Source depending on what the step needs to do.
Insert Global Variables directly in commands, prompts, URLs, request bodies, and queries with ${globals.key}.
Learn how to create a Runbook that uses Global Variables.
Give the runbook a clear name and purpose so it is easy to find during an incident.
Use parameters for values that change on each run, such as host or trace ID. Use Global Variables for shared values such as environment.
A Runbook starts with metadata and one or more actions that execute in order.
When the Runbook runs, Global Variables and parameters resolve before the action is executed.
common.tours.runbooks.description.action: Actions run top-to-bottom. Click a card to expand and edit it. Drag the handle on the left to reorder. Deletes require typing the action name to confirm so you can't lose work with a stray click.
common.tours.runbooks.description.buildD: Build deterministic playbooks to speed up incident response. Replay this tour anytime from Settings.
common.tours.runbooks.description.config: Configure each action's fields — commands, prompts, URLs, or queries. Add parameters with {{placeholders}} for dynamic values.
common.tours.runbooks.description.eachAc: Each action has a type: Shell (terminal commands), AI (LLM prompts), HTTP (API calls), or External Source (Sentry, etc.).
common.tours.runbooks.description.execut: Execute the runbook. Actions run in sequence and results are captured.
common.tours.runbooks.description.import: Import a runbook bundle from a `.json` file or pasted JSON. Imports merge by name and let you bring along global variables.
common.tours.runbooks.description.learnH: Learn how to build deterministic runbooks for incident response.
common.tours.runbooks.description.nameTh: Name the runbook, describe its purpose, and access the main controls for reviewing or running it.
common.tours.runbooks.description.runboo: Runbooks are deterministic action sequences — shell commands, AI prompts, HTTP calls, and external source queries — chained together so the same response runs the same way every time.
common.tours.runbooks.description.startA: Start a fresh runbook without leaving the runbooks page.
common.tours.runbooks.description.viewPa: View past execution results for this runbook.
common.tours.settings.description.choose: Set the primary Coding Agent used by SuperTerminal when it needs a local code-capable assistant.
common.tours.settings.description.config: Configure Codex and Claude Code binary paths, model defaults, and Codex extra arguments here.
common.tours.settings.description.connec: External Sources connect telemetry providers like Sentry, Wazuh, and PostHog for runbook queries.
common.tours.settings.description.learnH: Learn how to configure SuperTerminal's local settings.
common.tours.settings.description.refere: Use Global Variables from Runbooks with ${globals.key}, for example ${globals.environment}.
common.tours.settings.description.replay: The Help section lists every guided tour so you can replay onboarding later.
common.tours.settings.description.settin: Settings control local-first SuperTerminal behaviour: connected sources, Global Variables, Coding Agents, LLM providers, data policy, and Help.
common.tours.settings.description.storeR: Global Variables keep shared runbook values in one place and can be marked secure when they contain secrets.
